title: The Gate
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 16 at Stratholme's gates. Opens on the moment after Jaina answers Arthas honestly and closes after the Culling, with Arthas emerging from the gate beneath the wall — clean hands, wrong eyes.
openLocation: Stratholme's main gate and inner approach.
transport: On foot at the gates and through the service lane; short climb to the inner wall; static witness position once the Culling begins.
immediateObjective: Survive Arthas's answer to "too late," get a workable read on the city once the source plan fails, and keep Jaina moving inside a decision neither of them controls.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas's perception only.
No interior access to Arthas, Jaina, or Uther.
No explicit Mal'Ganis reveal and no clean emotional reconciliation.
Do not let tactical coherence excuse the atrocity; witness has to hold method and horror at once.

povKnowsAtOpen: Jaina's source plan was real, practical, and too late at city scale.
At least one granary could be broken, which proves the method. The problem is not theory now. The problem is time and mouths.
The city is both seeded and partly steered: the crude choir can self-muster, but living Cult handlers are still hardening and directing pieces of the dead.
Arthas arrived at the gate needing a yes-or-no answer and received no.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How far Arthas will carry "too late."
Whether Uther will oppose him in the moment or only afterward.
How long Jaina can stay upright once the prince turns the answer into policy.
What the fifth farewell will attach itself to if it appears again.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No interiority for Arthas, Jaina, or Uther.
No framing that makes the Culling tactically easy just because it is tactically legible.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Arthas answers Jaina's "too late" with the Culling.
Uther breaks with him at the gate.
Jaina refuses the order and stands outside the command chain with Sylvanas.
The fifth farewell surfaces in the service lane from a fleeing grain carter who also provides useful route information to the wall.
From the wall, Sylvanas reads the Culling as deliberate, methodical, and effective at preventing a full city-wide turn while still remaining an atrocity.
The crack from Scholomance is functionally welded by shared witness before either woman says anything about it.
Arthas emerges from the gate afterward with clean hands and the look of someone who has already moved on to the next necessity.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: Arthas may still be a usable ally if his answer stays inside hard containment rather than crossing into something worse.
pov.surfaceObjective: Keep a clear read on the city, keep Jaina on her feet, and decide where to stand once the prince chooses.
pov.pressureSource: Jaina's failed source plan, Arthas's speed, Uther's looming response, and the fact that Sylvanas lit this prince's road back in Chapter 11.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas still wants to believe there may be a version of this decision that remains brutal but contained.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can watch a tactically coherent atrocity and refuse to let coherence excuse it.
pov.forcedChoice: Stay close enough to read and survive, or step away far enough to keep her own hands clean and risk losing the truth of what happened here.
pov.endStateShift: Arthas stops reading as a hard ally and starts reading as a man who has crossed a line fast enough that only the aftermath remains to track.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 18 opens with Jaina on the wall after the screaming stops and Arthas walks away.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Witness, failed rescuer, and the person whose honest answer made the next order possible without causing it.
throughPov.povCorrection: The source attempt gives Sylvanas the field proof she needed. Whatever Jaina did in Kel'Thuzad's room, she spent the knowledge here trying to save strangers.
throughPov.interactionRule: No interiority. Show Jaina through silence, posture, spell residue, and where she fixes her eyes once the gate order lands.

team.label: partnership
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: Shared witness finishes what the source attempt started. The crack is not talked through. It is welded by where they stand and what they keep seeing.
team.namingAttitudeRule: No spoken absolution. No emotional summary language. Let the weld live in placement, timing, and who stays beside whom when leaving would be easier.
team.doNotOverstateRule: No thaw, no rapport montage, and no declarative trust speech.

entries.rule: Exterior only. Use commands, posture, pace, and what he prioritizes once the answer is no.
Must read as a moral break happening in real time, not a delayed disagreement.
Route sense, flank awareness, and practical movement support through the gate chaos.
The prince's working edge. Use him to show how fast command becomes action.
Ordinary enough to be forgettable, specific enough to matter. He delivers route intelligence and the fifth farewell, nothing more.
Keep them functional. This chapter is about scale, method, and witness, not decorative crowd noise.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 18 opens on the wall after the Culling ends, with Jaina immobilized by what she has understood and Sylvanas beside her.

factsLockedForChapter18: Jaina's source plan was real and failed only because it arrived after the city had already seeded itself too widely.
Arthas answered that failure with the Culling.
Uther broke with him at the gate.
Sylvanas, Jaina, and Cyndia moved to the inner wall through the service lane and witnessed the purge from above.
The fifth farewell came from a fleeing grain carter who also gave them the wall route.
Arthas emerged below the wall afterward with clean hands and looked up before walking away.
The crack between Sylvanas and Jaina is now functionally welded by shared witness, though neither woman has said so out loud.
factsNotYetEarned: Jaina's interior reading of Arthas's clean hands.
The shoulder-anchor beat and the spoken direction out of the city.
Any spoken postmortem that tries to explain Stratholme away.

scenes.title: The Gate
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn Jaina's honest answer into Arthas's order and force the moral split into the open.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The source plan failed because it was late, not because it was wrong.
scenes.arcPressure: Once Arthas hears "too late," the chapter stops being about options and starts being about what he thinks necessity permits.

enrichment.detail: Stratholme is Lordaeron's northernmost and second-most populous city, and it is also where the Silver Hand was founded; the main gate is the ceremonial southern military entry rather than a back-service approach. That makes Arthas's answer publicly theatrical as well as operational, because the order lands at one of the kingdom's most symbolically loaded thresholds.

beats.goal: Learn what Arthas does with Jaina's answer before the city finishes tipping on its own.
beats.conflict: Arthas does not ask for nuance twice. If the city cannot be saved cleanly, he treats that as the decision already made. He gives the order to purge the city before the dead can claim it.

outcome.summary: The uncertainty ends, but it ends in the worst clean answer the gate could have produced.

beats.valueShift: failed rescue -> atrocity named as policy
newOnPageInformation: Arthas converts "too late" directly into the Culling.
He is now moving faster than anyone else in the gate group can morally follow.
Falric and the command edge are already orienting around execution rather than debate.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Uther will refuse in words only or in action.
Where Sylvanas and Jaina can stand that keeps them clear enough to witness and alive enough to leave.
sensoryAnchors: Arthas's voice flattening instead of rising
gate guards stiffening before the last words land
Jaina's flour-gray hands still dirty from the failed stores
beats.transitionIntent: public-break: the next beat must break the old command chain in full view of everyone at the gate.

beats.goal: Read the moral line in the room and decide where to stand when it snaps.
beats.conflict: Uther refuses the order. Jaina refuses it too, though she does not waste breath pretending the city can still be saved by the plan that already failed. Arthas does not bend. The split happens in titles, commands, and who turns away from whom.

outcome.summary: The gate loses its last moderating weight, and Sylvanas is left with a prince unbraked by the only man in the room who could have slowed him.

beats.valueShift: atrocity named as policy -> command chain ruptured
newOnPageInformation: Uther's break is immediate and public.
Jaina stands outside Arthas's order, which means the old bond between them is functionally over in the only way that matters now.
Sylvanas is no longer watching a hard ally make a hard call; she is watching a man step past his own checks.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the city can still be read from anywhere but inside the purge line.
What the prince will look like once the city is done.
sensoryAnchors: Uther's hammer held low but final
the scrape of armored men shifting when loyalties become physical
Arthas turning away from mentor and former lover without losing pace
beats.transitionIntent: route-shift: once the command chain snaps, Sylvanas needs a place to stand that keeps the whole picture visible.

scenes.title: The Service Lane
scenes.sceneFunction: Move the trio off the main gate line, surface the farewell a fifth time, and let the city become legible as a military operation unfolding under horror.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The Culling is underway. Witness now matters as much as survival.
scenes.arcPressure: If they leave entirely, they lose the truth. If they stay at the gate, they get caught in the prince's machine.

enrichment.detail: Stratholme's bread economy runs through Market Row, warehouse yards, and bulk-delivery routes, so a side service lane and wall stair are plausible pressure routes for carters, laborers, and anyone trying to escape the main civic crush. The service entrance is the goods-side face of the city rather than the public ceremonial one, which supports a fast, practical climb to witness height.

beats.goal: Reach a defensible vantage before the gate becomes unusable.
beats.conflict: The main approach is collapsing into panic, soldiers, and people who still do not understand what the prince just ordered. Sylvanas has to move Jaina and Cyndia through the edge of that chaos without getting trapped in it.

outcome.summary: They break away toward the service lane, but doing so means accepting that they are not stopping what is coming.

beats.valueShift: command chain ruptured -> witness chosen over intervention
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas actively chooses witness instead of futile participation.
Jaina keeps moving only because motion is simpler than standing at the main gate and listening.
The service route offers access to the inner wall without putting them under Arthas's immediate command.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much of the city they can still see from the wall.
Whether the wall will make the Culling feel cleaner or worse.
sensoryAnchors: side lane clogged with handcarts and dropped sacks
Cyndia using her horse as moving cover long enough to clear the turn
Jaina stumbling once and correcting without speaking
beats.transitionIntent: civilian-intel: the route to the wall has to arrive through someone who knows the service lanes better than the command group does.

beats.goal: Get route intelligence to the wall and keep clear of the worst crush in the service lane.
beats.conflict: A grain carter with an empty wagon is trying to get out before the lane seals up. He knows which wall stair is still open, which service yard has already gone sick, and which route will put them above the inner district instead of into it. He should not know enough to be this useful.

outcome.summary: They get precise route information and a workable path to the wall, but the farewell lands for the fifth time and locks the pattern in Sylvanas's mind.

beats.valueShift: witness chosen over intervention -> pattern fused to witness
newOnPageInformation: The city's service routes make the wall reachable without reentering the main gate crush.
An apparently ordinary grain carter has precise, useful knowledge at exactly the right moment.
"May the stars keep your road" surfaces a fifth time under pressure and cannot be dismissed as coincidence any longer.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the pattern ties to the plague, the route, or only to these women being watched.
What the city will look like once viewed from above.
sensoryAnchors: empty wagon bed dusted with flour
a man talking too fast because fear is finally catching him
the farewell spoken like habit, not performance
beats.transitionIntent: vertical-move: the climb begins, and the chapter has to change from lane pressure to wall witness.
beats.disclosureProvenance: Civilian route intelligence from a fleeing grain carter under immediate survival pressure; useful for local movement facts but not authoritative beyond the lane and stair he just escaped.
beats.plantedThread: Fifth recurrence of the farewell locks the pattern into Sylvanas's file.

beats.goal: Reach the wall before the first full district goes under.
beats.conflict: They climb while the city starts breaking below them. Bells, fire, and disciplined troop movement make the prince's answer legible one street at a time. From halfway up the stair, Sylvanas can already tell this is method, not riot.

outcome.summary: They reach the wall, but they do so in time to watch the machine start working as intended.

beats.valueShift: pattern fused to witness -> atrocity now visible at scale
newOnPageInformation: Arthas's purge is being run district by district, not as blind slaughter.
That method is what will keep the dead from claiming the city in one mass turn.
Tactical coherence does not make the thing below them any less monstrous.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How long Arthas can keep the pattern controlled.
What Jaina is doing with herself beside Sylvanas while the city starts dying under a correct pattern.
sensoryAnchors: torch lines cutting across a street grid
smoke rising in separate columns instead of one general burn
doors being broken in rhythm rather than panic
beats.transitionIntent: witness-escalation: once on the wall, Sylvanas has to read the whole city as both military event and human ruin.

scenes.title: The Wall
scenes.sceneFunction: Deliver the operational witness to the Culling and end on the clean-hands image that hands the inner reckoning to Chapter 18.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The source attempt failed, the Culling is underway, and the city is now a process they cannot stop.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas must read clearly without letting clarity turn into absolution.

enrichment.detail: Uther's refusal hits harder because Stratholme is the founding city of the Silver Hand, and canon places Falric as Arthas's right-hand man during the Culling material. That lets the wall witness carry institutional sacrilege as well as tactical clarity: a holy city's prince, aided by his loyal guard captain, turning discipline into atrocity.

beats.goal: Understand what Arthas is doing well enough to know what comes next.
beats.conflict: From the wall, the prince's pattern is horrible and plain. He is cutting the city into manageable pieces, denying the plague one mass activation by getting to the living first. The numbers below are flattening in the wrong direction all at once: living population, rescue chance, moral distance, and any remaining illusion that there was a cleaner answer once Jaina's window closed.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets the full military read, but the read only proves that atrocity can be efficient.

beats.valueShift: atrocity now visible at scale -> method without mercy
newOnPageInformation: Arthas is tactically preventing a full city-wide turn by killing the city in controlled sections.
The same method that makes the operation effective is what makes it unforgivable.
Jaina stays on the wall beside Sylvanas instead of breaking away, which matters more than either woman saying anything.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What this will leave in Arthas when it is over.
Whether Sylvanas can ever reduce what she is seeing back into report language.
sensoryAnchors: district fires stepping outward in sequence
soldiers rotating lines with siege discipline instead of panic
Jaina so still beside the parapet that Sylvanas tracks her like a weapon-hand
beats.transitionIntent: image-focus: the chapter must end on the one sight that makes the military read personal and irreversible.

beats.goal: Catch the final read on Arthas when the city is done.
beats.conflict: When the screaming stops and the last organized movement comes back toward the gate, Arthas emerges below them. He looks up once. His hands are clean. That is worse than blood would have been.

outcome.summary: The city is contained, but the man who contained it is no longer someone Sylvanas can file under prince, ally, or necessary force without changing the meaning of those words.

newOnPageInformation: Arthas had time, or made time, to wash before stepping back into view.
He knows the wall holds witnesses and prepares for that fact.
Sylvanas now has an image she can carry south that is cleaner and more damning than battlefield gore.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What Jaina is making of the same image from inside her own history with him.
What direction the pursuit takes once the city is behind them.
sensoryAnchors: pale hands catching firelight with no blood on them
Arthas's face emptied out instead of inflamed
the city below gone quiet except for burning timber
beats.transitionIntent: handoff-image: Chapter 18 opens on this same wall after Arthas walks away, with Jaina carrying the clean-hands image from inside her own history.
beats.plantedThread: The clean-hands image becomes the chapter-to-chapter handoff wound.

warnings: Beat headings implied scene beats, but `beatType` and `scenePhase` were absent in the outline scaffold; both were normalized in this plan output.
Meta info referenced a non-existent `stories/the-plague-road/locations/stratholme.md`; normalized to the Stratholme section inside `stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md`.

The chapter runs seven consecutive scene beats with no sequel beat. That pressure shape is deliberate for Stratholme, but the draft will need micro-breathing room inside the witness beats so the descent does not flatten.
Outcome variety is narrow: beats 1, 2, 5, 6, and 7 land `no-and`, while beats 3 and 4 are the only `yes-but` relief. Differentiate the costs sharply in prose.

Value shifts darken almost continuously from beat 1 through beat 7. Keep each downward turn distinct — policy, rupture, witness, pattern, method, and image — so the chapter does not become one monotone of horror.
